def: The meridian, or middle line of the heavens; the point of theecliptic on the meridian.
To take by assault; to storm; to overcome; to vanquish; as, toexpugn cities; to expugn a person by arguments.
Of or pertaining to Poland or its inhabitants.-- n.
The quality or state of being starry; as, the starriness of theheavens.
Having no answer, or impossible to be answered. Byron.
A female beholder or looker-on. "A spectatress of the wholescene." Jeffrey.
Washed by the waves or tide; -- said of a rock or strip ofshore, or (Naut.) of an anchor, etc., when flush with the surface ofthe water, so that the waves break over it.
The office or position oaf an admiral; also, the naval skill ofan admiral.
A verbal or acted enigma based upon a word which has two ormore significant syllables or parts, each of which, as well as theword itself, is to be guessed from the descriptions orrepresentations.
